title: Adjustable swivel
abstract: A connection fitting (100) for fluidly coupling a fluid flow device to a fluid source includes a receiver (20) having a fluid inlet (22) configured to be fluidly coupled to the fluid source and an internal surface defining a reception chamber (24) fluidly coupled to the fluid inlet, a swivel member (30) disposed in the reception chamber of the receiver, the swivel member having a connection port (32) configured to be coupled to the fluid flow device and an external surface (36) including a substantially spherical external surface portion in contact with the internal surface of the receiver, and a restriction member (40) coupled to the receiver such that the swivel member is positioned between the receiver and the restriction member. The restriction member has a surface geometry defining a passageway (48) through which the fluid flow device can extend, and the surface geometry limits rotation of the swivel member within the receiver.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A connection fitting for fluidly coupling a fluid flow device to a fluid source, the connection fitting comprising: a receiver having a fluid inlet configured to be fluidly coupled to the fluid source and an internal surface defining a reception chamber fluidly coupled to the fluid inlet; a swivel member disposed in the reception chamber of the receiver, the swivel member having a connection port configured to be coupled to the fluid flow device and an external surface including a substantially spherical external surface portion in contact with the internal surface of the receiver; and a restriction member coupled to the receiver such that the swivel member is positioned between the receiver and the restriction member, the restriction member having a surface geometry defining a passageway through which the fluid flow device can extend, the surface geometry limiting rotation of the swivel member within the receiver. 2. The connection fitting of Claim 1, further comprising a securement member engaged with the receiver to at least selectively couple the restriction member to the receiver. 3. The connection fitting of Claim 2, wherein the securement member is configured to selectively force the restriction member toward the receiver, thereby selectively fixing an orientation of the restriction member relative to the receiver. 4. The connection fitting of Claim 1, wherein the surface geometry of the restriction member limits rotation of the swivel member to (a) rotation about a central axis of the receiver and (b) rotation about a second axis extending perpendicular to the central axis. 5. The connection fitting of Claim 4, wherein the external surface of the swivel member includes a first planar portion and the restriction member includes a second planar portion in contact with the first planar portion to limit rotation of the swivel member relative to the restriction member. 6. The connection fitting of Claim 5, wherein the swivel member is configured to rotate about the central axis through a maximum rotation angle of at least 360 degrees, wherein the connection port is centered about and extends along a connection port axis, wherein a bend angle is defined between the connection port axis and the central axis, wherein the swivel member is configured to rotate about the second axis such that the bend angle is adjustable from zero degrees to a maximum bend angle, and wherein the maximum bend angle ranges from greater than thirty degrees to less than sixty degrees. 7. The connection fitting of Claim 6, wherein the maximum bend angle ranges from greater than thirty degrees to fifty degrees. 8. The connection fitting of Claim 1, wherein the swivel member defines an internal passageway in fluid communication with the connection port and having an inlet. 9. The connection fitting of Claim 8, wherein the internal passageway of the swivel member is skewed with respect to the connection port of the swivel member. 10. The connection fitting of Claim 9, wherein when the connection port of the swivel member is axially aligned with a central axis of the receiver, the inlet of the internal passageway of the swivel member defines a first cross-sectional area through which fluid can flow, and wherein when the connection port is skewed with respect to the central axis, the inlet of the internal passageway of the swivel member defines a second cross-sectional area through which fluid can flow that is the same as the first cross-sectional area. 11. A connection fitting for fluidly coupling a fluid flow device to a fluid source, the connection fitting comprising: a receiver having a first inlet, a first outlet, and a first internal passageway extending between the first inlet and the first outlet, the receiver including a reception chamber centered about a first axis, the reception chamber being in fluid communication with the first internal passageway; a swivel member disposed in the reception chamber of the receiver, the swivel member having a second inlet fluidly coupled to the first outlet, a second outlet, and a second internal passageway extending between the second inlet and the second outlet, the swivel member including a connection port centered about a second axis and configured to be coupled to the fluid flow device, the connection port being downstream of the second outlet and in fluid communication with the second internal passageway; and a restriction member having an internal surface configured to engage an external surface of the swivel member to permit rotation of the swivel member relative to the restriction member about only a third axis that intersects and is perpendicular to the first axis. 12. The connection fitting of Claim 11, wherein over 50% of the external surface of the swivel member is partially spherical about a common center point. 13. The connection fitting of Claim 12, wherein the external surface of the swivel member includes a first planar portion and the internal surface of the restriction member includes a second planar portion in contact with the first planar portion to restrict rotation of the swivel member with respect to the restriction member. 14. The connection fitting of Claim 11, further comprising a securement member coupling the restriction member to the receiver, wherein the securement member is configured to limit rotation of the restriction member to rotation about the first axis. 15. The connection fitting of Claim 14, wherein the swivel member is configured to rotate about the first axis through a maximum rotation angle of at least 360 degrees with respect to the receiver. 16. The connection fitting of Claim 15, wherein a bend angle is defined between the first axis and the second axis, wherein the bend angle is adjustable from zero degrees to a maximum bend angle, and wherein the maximum bend angle ranges from greater than thirty degrees to less than sixty degrees. 17. The connection fitting of Claim 16, wherein the maximum bend angle ranges from greater than thirty degrees to fifty degrees. 18. The connection fitting of Claim 14, wherein the securement member is configured to selectively fixedly couple the restriction member and the swivel member to the receiver. 19. The connection fitting of Claim 11, wherein the second internal passageway of the swivel member is centered about and extends along a fourth axis that is skewed with respect to the second axis of the swivel member. 20. A method of swivel adjustment for a fluid distribution device comprising: providing a main body defining a first internal passageway configured to be fluidly coupled to a fluid source, the main body defining a reception chamber fluidly coupled to the first internal passageway; providing a swivel member within the reception chamber such that a second internal passageway of the swivel member is fluidly coupled to the first internal passageway, wherein the swivel member is configured to be coupled to the fluid distribution device such that the fluid distribution device is fluidly coupled to the second internal passageway, and wherein the swivel member is configured to rotate with respect to the main body; adjusting an orientation of the swivel member, wherein the swivel member is adjustable through at least 360 degrees about one axis and through a total inclusive angle of articulation of up to 100 degrees with respect to the one axis; and selectively securing the swivel member in a desired orientation.
